Chief Complaint
Neck and back pain following MVC.
HPI
Patient presents via private vehicle with neck and low back pain following a rear-end MVA earlier today. Impact was
significant. Patient denies prior neck pain or back issues. No LOC reported.

• Eyes: Normal.
• MSK: Neck pain.

Vertebral Bodies: Vertebral body heights are preserved. No acute fracture.

Soft Tissues: Prevertebral soft tissues are unremarkable.

• No acute fracture or dislocation.
• No significant degenerative changes.

Page 1
MISC
Office Visit
Subjective: Patient follows up for neck and back pain after MVA. Pain is 6/10.

Plan: Referral to Physical Therapy. Modified duty: no lifting > 10 lbs. Follow up in 4 weeks.
Work Status: Modified Duty: No lifting > 10lbs.

Elite Physical Therapy
Patient: Alexander Hill (MRN126225) | Date: 2025-04-17
Page 1
PT RECORDS
INITIAL EVALUATION
History of Present Illness:
Patient presents for evaluation of chronic neck and back pain following a motor vehicle accident.
Functional Status:
Patient reports difficulty with ADLs including dressing and grooming. Sitting tolerance limited to 20 minutes.
Physical Examination:
Cervical ROM: Flexion 30 deg, Extension 20 deg. Lumbar ROM: Flexion 45 deg. Strength 4/5 in bilateral UE.

Clinical Assessment:
Patient demonstrates significant deficits in ROM and strength consistent with cervical/lumbar strain.
Plan of Care:
2-3x/week

Page 1
PT RECORDS
DAILY TREATMENT NOTE - 2025-04-21
Subjective:
Patient reports pain levels at 8/10. Tolerance to treatment is poor. Noted increased
paraspinal spasm after activities.
Objective:
Palpation reveals guarded movement in mixed region. ROM limited by pain.
Assessment:
Patient is stable. Tolerating treatment.
Plan:
Continue with current plan of care.
